summary:
The most common cause of interdental papilla protrusion beneath a rubber dam is improperly placed dental punches, specifically when positioned too closely together, which mechanically displaces the papillae due to insufficient soft tissue support. Drug-induced gingival overgrowth, while associated with enlarged papillae, is a chronic systemic condition unrelated to acute procedural disruptions seen during restorative dentistry.  

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{D}}$
